message:
refactor(extensions): recover from poisoned mutexes instead of panicking

Replace ~95 occurrences of .lock().expect("lock error") with
.lock().unwrap_or_else(std::sync::PoisonError::into_inner) across
NativeRustRuntime, ExtensionManager, EventCoalescer, and test code.

This changes the mutex poisoning strategy from "panic with a generic
message" (crashing the entire process on any subsequent lock attempt after
a thread panic) to "recover gracefully by extracting the inner data." For
an agent application, partial state recovery is preferable to a hard crash
since the poisoned mutex typically holds non-critical UI, telemetry, or
policy-cache state where inconsistency is tolerable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
